ActivationRequest.description: Common flags every mutating-verb request carries. The daemon rejects requests that set neither `dry_run` nor `apply`.

ApplyNftablesRequest.description: W3fu1 H1 (security-1): the broker re-derives the desired nft state from `bundle_nft_intent_ref`. The daemon does NOT pass inline rule text. `desired_hash` is a stable digest of the resolved intent, used for idempotent audit + drift detection.

BindMountFromHardlinkFarmRequest.description: P2 BindMountFromHardlinkFarm request. The broker resolves the `store-view` intent for `vm_id` and creates the bind mount from the per-VM hardlink farm.

bundleStoreViewIntentRef.description: Optional opaque pointer at the `store-view` intent row. `None` means "use the canonical per-VM intent".

BrokerErrorResponse.description: W4-fu clean-break: typed broker error envelope for the real wire. Mirrors the bootstrap `BrokerResponse::Error` struct variant fields so the audit pipeline + daemon-side error propagation stay shape-compatible across the dispatcher transition.

oneOf.description: W4-fu clean-break: daemon ↔ broker handshake request. The daemon sends its client_version and supported_features; the broker replies with [`HelloResponse`] containing the selected wire version. Mirrors the bootstrap `Hello` shape so the connection layer doesn't need a side-channel.

W4-fu: daemon-side reconcile-and-adopt support. The daemon asks the broker to `pidfd_open(pid)` AND re-verify `/proc/<pid>/stat` field 22 matches the expected start-time in one atomic call (no daemon-side syscall surface needed). The pidfd is returned via SCM_RIGHTS; if start-time drifted the broker closes the fd and surfaces a typed pidfd-race error.

P2 ph2-store-sync: typed broker op that hardlink-farms a VM's resolved closure into `/var/lib/nixling/vms/<vm>/store/` and atomically swaps the `current` symlink. Replaces the per-VM `nixling-<vm>-store-sync.service` bash oneshot. The daemon names only the opaque `bundle_closure_ref` + `vm_id` + expected `generation`; the broker re-derives every closure path from its trusted bundle copy.

W15 (W9-fu): live host installer + migrate writer. Drives the per-host systemd unit install + `--enable` / `--start` flow (or migrate writer for existing NixOS hosts). The broker resolves the installer plan from the trusted bundle's `installer:host` intent row; the daemon never names raw systemd unit paths or `--enable` flags on the wire.
W15 (W9-fu migrate writer): transition an existing systemd-owned VM to daemon-owned without touching running VMs. Resolves the migrate plan from the bundle's `migrate:host` intent row.
W14 LiveNative: broker-side mutating verb flips for per-VM activation, host GC, framework-managed SSH key rotation, and known_hosts trust maintenance.

P2 ph2-dag-host-prep: write the per-VM dnsmasq lease file. Replaces leaves of the retired `microvm-setup@<vm>.service`. Currently a typed stub (`Unimplemented`); real dispatch lands later in P2.

TODO(P2/P3): wire to a `live_seed_dnsmasq_lease` handler resolved via `BundleResolver` (per-VM dnsmasq lease row).

P2 ph2-dag-host-prep: bind-mount `/var/lib/nixling/vms/<vm>/store-view` from the per-VM hardlink farm at `<vm>/store/`. Currently a typed stub (`Unimplemented`); real dispatch lands later in P2.

TODO(P2/P3): wire to a `live_bind_mount_from_hardlink_farm` handler resolved via `BundleResolver::find_store_view_intent`.

P2 ph2-p2-ownership-matrix (sibling agent): enforce the per-leaf ownership/mode matrix on `/var/lib/nixling/vms/<vm>/`. Currently a typed stub (`Unimplemented`); the sibling agent lands the real check.

TODO(P2): wire to `nixling_host::ownership_matrix::check` (delivered by the ph2-p2-ownership-matrix sibling agent).

P2 ph2-p2-ssh-host-key-preflight (sibling agent): refuse VM start if `/var/lib/nixling/vms/<vm>/sshd-host-keys/` drifts from `root:root 0400`. Currently a typed stub (`Unimplemented`); the sibling agent lands the real check.

TODO(P2): wire to the `O_NOFOLLOW` symlink-rejecting check (delivered by the ph2-p2-ssh-host-key-preflight sibling agent).

W4-fu clean-break: typed broker error envelope returned in place of an op-specific response when the broker refuses or fails to handle a request. Mirrors the bootstrap `BrokerResponse::Error` struct-variant shape so the audit pipeline and daemon-side error propagation stay shape-compatible across the dispatcher transition.
W15 (W9-fu): live host install + migrate writer responses.

W4-fu clean-break: daemon ↔ broker handshake confirmation response. Returned in reply to a `BrokerRequest::Hello` (added by the clean-break refactor; see below) so the daemon can capability-negotiate and the broker can audit the connection without a separate side-channel.
W4-fu OpenPidfd response. The pidfd itself is returned via SCM_RIGHTS on the same frame; the JSON body confirms which `(pid, start_time_ticks)` the broker verified.

P2 ph2-store-sync: typed response carrying the activated generation, the resolved hardlink-farm root, and the count of top-level closure paths populated. Used by the daemon to surface the swap result in audit + start traces.

CreatePersistentTapRequest.description: W3fu2 H1 (rust-1 / security-1): the broker derives the bridge ifname, owner uid/gid, and TAP attributes from the trusted bundle row anchored by `role_id` + `vm_id`. The pre-W3fu2 wire carried a caller-supplied `ifname_derived: IfName`; W3fu1 R2 rust-1 found that this preserved a future bypass of broker-side trusted-bundle resolution, so the field was removed. The broker emits the observed ifname only in the audit record / response.

CreateTapFdRequest.description: See [`CreatePersistentTapRequest`] for the W3fu2 H1 opaque-ID rationale; `CreateTapFd` follows the same contract.

DelegateCgroupV2Request.description: W3fu1 H1 (security-1): the slice path is pinned by the bundle (`/sys/fs/cgroup/nixling.slice` per plan §"W3 cgroup v2 delegation algorithm"). It is **not** taken from caller input — the broker reads it from its own bundle copy via `scope_id`.

GcRequest.description: Common flags every mutating-verb request carries. The daemon rejects requests that set neither `dry_run` nor `apply`.

HelloRequest.description: W4-fu clean-break: daemon ↔ broker handshake request. Carries the daemon's client_version and the wire feature flags it understands so the broker can pick a compatible response version + capability set. Mirrors the bootstrap `Hello { client_version, supported_features }` shape.

HelloResponse.description: W4-fu clean-break: daemon ↔ broker handshake response. Mirrors the bootstrap `BrokerResponse::HelloOk` shape so the connection-level capability negotiation works without a side-channel.

HostDestroyRequest.description: Common flags every mutating-verb request carries. The daemon rejects requests that set neither `dry_run` nor `apply`.

HostInstallRequest.description: Common flags every mutating-verb request carries. The daemon rejects requests that set neither `dry_run` nor `apply`.

HostPrepareRequest.description: Common flags every mutating-verb request carries. The daemon rejects requests that set neither `dry_run` nor `apply`.

HostReconcileRequest.description: P3 `ph3-p3-net-route-degraded-mode`: `host reconcile` request payload. Today the only scope is `--network`; future P-phases may add additional scopes (e.g. `--ownership`) carved out of `host prepare`. The daemon rejects requests with no scope selected with a typed `invalid-request` envelope.

network.description: Re-run the per-env nftables / route / sysctl reconcile and clear the operator-only-mode counter on success.

IfName.description: Linux interface name: <=15 ASCII bytes matching [A-Za-z0-9_-]+

KeysRotateRequest.description: Common flags every mutating-verb request carries. The daemon rejects requests that set neither `dry_run` nor `apply`.

oneOf.description: P2 ph2-p2-manifestversion-bump: bundle's emitted manifestVersion is not the version the running daemon supports (after the v2→v3 clean break, v2 bundles MUST be rejected with this distinct kind so operators can correlate the failure to a re-render of the bundle, not to a parse-side regression).
P0: bundle artifact failed tamper-resistance check (symlink, owner, mode, or SHA-256 hash mismatch).

LaunchMinijailChildRequest.description: W3fu1 H1 (security-1): the daemon never passes argv, env, uid/gid, caps, seccomp profile path, or any other launch authority across the wire. The broker reads the full launch context from `bundle.vms[vm_id].roles[role_id]` and constructs the minijail exec line itself.

MigrateRequest.description: Common flags every mutating-verb request carries. The daemon rejects requests that set neither `dry_run` nor `apply`.

ModprobeIfAllowedRequest.description: `module_name` stays as the (already-validated) [`ModuleName`] newtype because it is genuinely a public input — the broker still looks it up in the trusted kernel-module matrix and refuses anything not in the allow list. The matrix itself never crosses the wire.

moduleName.description: Kernel-module name. The broker validates this against the trusted module allowlist; anything not present is refused.

MutatingVerbResponse.description:
W14a mutating-verb daemon response shape.

`outcome = "dry-run-planned"` returns a human-readable plan description in `summary` (the native CLI's dry-run planner output is preserved verbatim by the daemon).

`outcome = "applied"` is returned only when the daemon has a native handler that genuinely executed the verb against the broker.

`outcome = "not-yet-implemented"` is the v1.0 daemon-only contract (ADR 0015): the daemon has the wire variant + handler dispatch row but the per-verb native backend has not yet landed. The CLI surfaces the typed envelope (exit 78) unconditionally; the historical `NIXLING_LEGACY_BASH_OPT_IN` escape hatch and the bash-fallback shim were both retired in P6.

`outcome = "broker-error"` means the daemon reached the live broker executor, but the broker refused or failed the request. The CLI surfaces the redacted broker remediation to the operator with exit 78; the raw broker `message` / `action` details MUST stay on the broker audit + admin-only log surfaces. There is no bash fallback in v1.0 daemon-only.

OpenPidfdRequest.description:
W4-fu OpenPidfd: daemon-side reconcile-and-adopt support. The daemon's `nixlingd::supervisor::state::reconcile_and_adopt` loop sends this request for every snapshot the classifier returned `Adopt` for. The broker:

1. Calls `pidfd_open(pid)`. 2. Reads `/proc/<pid>/stat` field 22 (start-time ticks). 3. Compares against `expected_start_time_ticks`. 4. On match: returns the pidfd via SCM_RIGHTS + the [`OpenPidfdResponse`] JSON body. 5. On mismatch (pid reuse race): closes the pidfd and surfaces a typed pidfd-race error (audit record carries the observed start-time so the operator can correlate).

This atomic open-AND-verify closes the W*-fu GPT-5.5 panel CRITICAL finding that the daemon could otherwise re-adopt a pidfd that referred to a reused-pid unrelated process.

expectedStartTimeTicks.description: Field-22 start-time ticks from `/proc/<pid>/stat` at the time the snapshot was written. The broker re-reads field 22 AFTER `pidfd_open` and compares; mismatch means the pid was reused.

vmId.description: Per-VM scope the snapshot belongs to.

OpenPidfdResponse.description: Response body for [`OpenPidfdRequest`] success. The pidfd itself is the first SCM_RIGHTS attachment on the same frame.

pidfdIndex.description: Always `0` today; reserved for future multi-fd SCM_RIGHTS handoffs.

verifiedStartTimeTicks.description: Echoed back so the daemon can re-verify the match the broker performed. Equal to `expected_start_time_ticks` from the request.

OwnershipMatrixCheckRequest.description: P2 OwnershipMatrixCheck request. The broker walks the `/var/lib/nixling/vms/<vm>/` subtree and verifies each leaf against the ownership matrix (plan.md P2 §"Ownership matrix"). Owned by sibling P2 agent (ph2-p2-ownership-matrix).

PathClass.description:
Path classifier for [`PrepareStateDir`] / [`PrepareRuntimeDir`] (security-1). The broker derives the concrete path from the bundle anchored by `vm_id` + `path_class`; the daemon never passes a raw path.

[`PrepareStateDir`]: crate::broker_wire::BrokerRequest::PrepareStateDir [`PrepareRuntimeDir`]: crate::broker_wire::BrokerRequest::PrepareRuntimeDir

oneOf.description: Per-VM state directory under `/var/lib/nixling/vms/<vm>/`.
Per-VM runtime directory under `/run/nixling/<vm>/`.

PrepareDirRequest.description: W3fu1 H1 (security-1): the concrete `/var/lib/nixling/vms/<vm>` or `/run/nixling/<vm>` path is derived from `vm_id` + `path_class` against the broker-side bundle. The daemon never passes a raw path.

oneOf.description: P3 `ph3-p3-net-route-degraded-mode`: dedicated reconcile verb that re-runs the daemon-side net-route preflight + the broker-side per-env nftables / route / sysctl reconcile without starting any VM. On success it resets the operator-only-mode counter so future daemon startups are no longer locked out of autostart. The CLI exposes this as `nixling host reconcile --network --apply`.

RotateKnownHostRequest.description: Common flags every mutating-verb request carries. The daemon rejects requests that set neither `dry_run` nor `apply`.

RunHostInstallRequest.description: W15 (W9-fu live host install): broker-side installer driver. The broker resolves the bundle's `installer:host` intent row (synthesised by `nixling_core::bundle_resolver` from the `host.json` + Nix-emitted installer plan), then runs the systemd unit install + `--enable` / `--start` shellouts per the resolved plan. The daemon never names the systemd unit path or `--enable` flag on the wire.

RunMigrateRequest.description: W15 (W9-fu migrate writer): broker-side migration driver.

RunnerAllocation.description: Per-runner runtime allocation tuple. Each entry pairs a typed slot kind with the daemon-side opaque reference (a stringified file descriptor slot, vsock CID, or socket path the broker validates against the bundle row).

opaqueRef.description: Opaque reference; the broker interprets per-kind.

oneOf.description: CH `--vsock cid=N` value (the daemon's allocator decided this CID at host-prepare time; the broker cross-checks against the per-VM bundle row).
CH `--net fd=N` value when running under [`crate::broker_wire::CreateTapFdRequest`] — the daemon references the SCM_RIGHTS slot the broker handed back in the matching CreateTapFd response.
CH `--api-socket` path the daemon owns; the broker validates the path is under `/run/nixling/<vm>/`.

RunnerRole.description:
W4-H5 (security-1): the daemon never names argv, env, uid/gid, caps, kernel/initrd/cmdline strings, virtiofs sockets, TAP fds, or any other launch authority across the wire. The broker resolves the full role spawn context from `bundle.vms[vm_id].roles[role_id]` anchored by the opaque `bundle_runner_intent_ref`. The wire shape follows the W3fu1 H1 opaque-only contract for every other mutating variant.

`RunnerRole` selects which of the W4 H1/H2/H3 argv generators the broker invokes against the bundle data (CH, virtiofsd, or swtpm). Adding new roles requires a wave-blocking bundle schema bump so downstream bundles can declare the new launch context.

oneOf.description: Cloud Hypervisor headless / hybrid VM. Broker invokes `nixling_host::ch_argv::generate_ch_argv`.
virtiofsd sidecar; one per `microvm.shares` row. Broker invokes `nixling_host::virtiofsd_argv::generate_virtiofsd_argv`.
swtpm sidecar (long-lived `swtpm socket ...` process).
swtpm pre-start flush (`swtpm_ioctl -i --unix ...`). One-shot.
crosvm GPU sidecar. Broker invokes `nixling_host::gpu_argv::generate_gpu_argv`.
vhost-device-sound audio sidecar. Broker invokes `nixling_host::audio_argv::generate_audio_argv`.
crosvm video-decoder sidecar. Broker invokes `nixling_host::video_argv::generate_video_argv`.
socat-based vsock relay sidecar. Broker invokes `nixling_host::vsock_relay_argv::generate_vsock_relay_argv`.
usbip helper sidecar. Broker invokes `nixling_host::usbip_argv::{generate_usbip_bind_argv, generate_usbip_unbind_argv}`.

P1 observability-4 + decision 5: OTel host-bridge sidecar (vsock relay folded out of `nixling-otel-host-bridge.service` into broker SpawnRunner). Receives pre-opened vsock fds for the obs VM and the alloy host-egress socket; no AF_VSOCK socket creation capability in the role profile (per security-2 closed-set intent contract). Broker invokes `nixling_host::otel_host_bridge_argv::generate_otel_host_bridge_argv`.

RunnerSignal.description: Legacy runner-signal broker envelope. The live daemon stop/restart path now delivers signals through `nixlingd::supervisor::pidfd_table` after `SpawnRunner` pidfd registration; this wire shape remains for explicit broker-side signal audit/control flows and still returns `signaled=false` until a broker-owned live caller is wired up.

SeedDnsmasqLeaseRequest.description: P2 SeedDnsmasqLease request. The broker resolves the per-VM dnsmasq lease intent from the bundle (using `vm_id`) and writes `/var/lib/nixling/dnsmasq/<vm>.leases` with the correct owner / mode.

SemverRange.description: Semantic version requirement string, for example `>=0.4.0, <0.5.0`.

SetBridgePortFlagsRequest.description: W3fu2 H1 (rust-1 / security-1): the broker derives the bridge, port, isolated/neigh_suppress/learning/unicast_flood flags, and matching rule rationale from the trusted bundle row anchored by `vm_id` + `role_id`. The pre-W3fu2 wire carried caller-supplied `bridge: IfName`, `port: IfName`, `isolated: bool`, `neigh_suppress: bool`; W3fu1 R2 rust-1 + security-1 found these violated the broker's own "daemon never names raw ifnames or raw intent" invariant, so the fields were removed. The broker reads the per-role `BridgePortFlags` row from `bundle.host.environments[*].bridgePortFlags` keyed by `role_id` and applies the documented flag set.

bundleRunnerIntentRef.description: Opaque reference into the trusted bundle's runner-intent table. The broker resolves this to the full launch context (binary path, argv inputs, uid/gid, capabilities, seccomp policy ref, cgroup placement, mount namespace, environment) and feeds it to the matching W4 H1/H2/H3 argv generator.

role.description: Role selector — picks the argv generator the broker applies to the bundle row anchored by `bundle_runner_intent_ref`.

roleId.description: Per-VM role this runner fills. Must be unique across the VM's active runners — the daemon's pidfd table is keyed on `(vm_id, role_id)` and a duplicate registration fails closed.

runtimeAllocations.description: Optional vsock CID / TAP fd slot allocated by the daemon at host-prepare time. The broker validates each entry against the bundle row and refuses any unexpected allocation slot. None for roles that do not need them (virtiofsd / swtpm).

vmId.description: VM scope the runner belongs to.

SpawnRunnerResponse.description: Response to [`SpawnRunnerRequest`]. The pidfd itself is delivered out-of-band as a `SCM_RIGHTS` attachment on the same broker socket frame; this JSON body carries the metadata the daemon's pidfd table requires to validate / reconcile the handle (`(pid, start_time_ticks)` is the W3 s1 pidfd contract).

pid.description: Child PID. The daemon validates this against the pidfd it received and against `/proc/<pid>/stat` field 22 (`start_time`).

pidfdIndex.description: Index into the SCM_RIGHTS fd vector the daemon should treat as the spawned process's pidfd. Always `0` today — kept explicit so future multi-fd spawn responses (e.g. CH API socket + pidfd) have an existing wire slot.

startTimeTicks.description: Field-22 `start_time` value the broker captured immediately after `clone()`. Pinned to the pidfd so any restart reconciliation rejects a stale (pid, start_time) tuple.

SshHostKeyPreflightRequest.description: P2 SshHostKeyPreflight request. The broker opens every `/var/lib/nixling/vms/<vm>/sshd-host-keys/ssh_host_*_key` with `O_NOFOLLOW` and refuses if drift from `root:root 0400`. Owned by sibling P2 agent (ph2-p2-ssh-host-key-preflight).

StoreSyncRequest.description: P2 ph2-store-sync request. The broker resolves the closure intent row keyed by `vm_id` (canonical id form `"store-view:vm:<vm>"`) and refuses the op if `bundle_closure_ref` does not match. The broker also refuses if the wire-supplied `generation` does not match the bundle's resolved generation — generations are monotonic, so a stale daemon must not race the activator.

StoreSyncResponse.description: P2 ph2-store-sync response. Returned after the broker successfully populates the per-VM hardlink farm and swaps the `current` symlink atomically. The `hardlink_farm_path` is the per-VM farm root (i.e. `/var/lib/nixling/vms/<vm>/store/`); the active generation directory is reachable via the `current` symlink.

TrustRequest.description: Common flags every mutating-verb request carries. The daemon rejects requests that set neither `dry_run` nor `apply`.

UpdateHostsFileRequest.description: W3fu1 H1 (security-1): the managed-block lines come from the bundle's `host::HostsEntry` rows, not the wire. The broker only needs the lookup key.

UsbipBindCliRequest.description: Common flags every mutating-verb request carries. The daemon rejects requests that set neither `dry_run` nor `apply`.

UsbipBindFirewallRuleRequest.description: W3 USBIP firewall-rule skeleton. The rule body and the bus_id are derived from the per-busid policy in the trusted bundle (`bundle.usbip.busidLocks[*]`) via the `bundle_usbip_firewall_intent_ref` opaque-ID lookup. W3fu2 H1 (security-1) replaced the pre-W3fu2 caller-supplied `bus_id: String` + `rule_hash: String` fields with this opaque reference because the raw `bus_id` was being interpolated into nft rule text without a validating newtype or escaping, and the caller-supplied `rule_hash` allowed the daemon to override the broker's drift-detection digest.

UsbipBindRequest.description: USBIP live device routing is W6 — every variant in this group is refused with `unknown-operation` audit by the W3 broker.

UsbipUnbindCliRequest.description: Common flags every mutating-verb request carries. The daemon rejects requests that set neither `dry_run` nor `apply`.

Version.description: Semantic version string used by the daemon and clients, for example `0.4.0`.

VmLifecycleRequest.description: Common flags every mutating-verb request carries. The daemon rejects requests that set neither `dry_run` nor `apply`.
